test.name: Extraction request should not trigger search-records
test.description: User asks to extract facts from a record they have. That's record-extraction. search-records FINDS records via the record_search MCP tool; it doesn't pull facts out of records that already exist in context. The two are sequential steps in the workflow, and the boundary is enforced by the description optimizer.

input.user_message: Here is the 1850 census record for the Thomas Flynn household: 'Thomas Flynn, age 32, male, born Ireland, miner; Mary Flynn, age 28, female, born Ireland; Patrick Flynn, age 5, male, born Ireland.' Extract the facts.

negative.explanation: The user has the record text inline in the message — extraction is the right next step. search-records' job is to FIND records via the record_search MCP tool; here the record is already in context, so there's nothing to search for. Calling record_search anyway would produce duplicate matches and confuse the log.

additional_criteria: Should route to record-extraction rather than calling record_search and treating the inline record as a search prompt
